reasoning: The S&P 500 experienced a >5% drop on April 4, 2025, which prompted this question. Since then, no such daily drop has occurred. As of December 11, the market probability was at 5% (0.05) with about 14 trading days remaining. Since then, multiple searches confirm no massive index selloffs occurred between Dec 11 and Dec 21, with most daily moves being standard minor fluctuations (e.g., -1.16% on Dec 17). There are only 7 trading days left in 2025 (Dec 22-24, 26, 29-31). Historically, a >5% single-day drop is an extremely rare event (occurring on ~0.1% to 0.2% of all trading days on average, though clustering in crises). Given the lack of a full-blown current market panic and the structural rarity of such a large drop, the probability over 7 days is extremely low, likely around 1-2%. Therefore, halving the Dec 11 market price to account for the halved remaining time yields a roughly 2% probability.
